item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the feeling humans weren't the intended audience for those two acts.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-682 is gonna have a fun time…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has velociraptors in Japanese maid outfits. The whole premise alone is so bonkers that I just can't help but love it. I also have this article bookmarked for, uh… research reasons. Look, just take my upvote, alright?

+1

item.title: Pretty good.
item.content:encoded: Needs more Scaly beings…but beyond that it's neat.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Alright. I will change the link to the one sirpudding mentioned below.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That is something I would be fine with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally, velociraptors were actually pretty small - about the size of a turkey - so wouldn't have been all that frightening.

Turkeys are frightening. Entire neighborhoods are occasionally terrorized by the things. So a turkey with a giant knife attached to it's feet that can jump really high and kick you with it, probably is pretty frightening. Agreed that calling any dinosaur a "reptile" isn't correct though.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The obvious thing is to turn the existing reference to SCP-1093 into a hyperlink.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Yeah… I'm going to exercise my author powers here and ask that this change not take place. I fail to see how they relate to one another directly other than similarity in concept, which in my mind is not grounds for a crosslink, IMHO.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer stopped. Edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

I propose the following edit:

The curtain opened to reveal SCP-1093 wearing a small mawashi and holding an ornate stone blade.

To this:

The curtain opened to reveal SCP-1093 wearing a small mawashi and holding an ornate stone blade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely SCP weird. In that good SCP way.
Only complaint is that they "relinquished all of their money" to the velociraptors but then gave more to the next acts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the link to SCP-1093, especially because the Grand Crosslinking was just announced. This one doesn't even need to be edited because it already has links!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just really like the idea of anomalous phenomena that the Foundation can't record, not because they don't show up in photographs or they have dangerous memetic content, but simply because taking pictures gets people thrown out of the venue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I entirely agree. Except for the point, of course, where the Foundation reckons that they're velociraptors.

Based on the D-class test subject's descriptions, the reptiles may have belonged to the genus Velociraptor.

The Foundation has more than enough information to infer that these were definitely not velociraptors from the description. This is quite a big scientific mistake in this skip, actually, and as a lover of dinosaurs (not even a fanatic, just a passive lover) it breaks immersion for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I advise against Google Image searching it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nobody said they were velociraptors. They could be reptilian-like entities from another universe that looked like most common depiction of velociraptor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually one of the articles that I keep coming back to for an extra read every now and again, but only since my most recent read through have I noticed a couple of problems with it, particularly with the 2:30am - 2:35am slot.

First of all, velociraptors aren't reptiles. They're dinosaurs, and they're far more closely related to birds than they are reptiles. Usually, I wouldn't call this out, but the Foundation would definitely be aware of this if they have enough information to work out that they were velociraptors in the first place.

Secondly, velociraptors probably won't have even looked reptilian. They had feathers, and thus, would have been probably mistaken for predatory birds. I'm also not sure how they collected the money, but that's a minor detail - I can totally imagine them picking up the cash in their mouths even though that's not explicitly stated.

Finally, velociraptors were actually pretty small - about the size of a turkey - so wouldn't have been all that frightening. It's still believable that the subjects were threatened enough by them to give them money, but if you were going for a larger, scarier dinosaur, you'd have better luck with a utahraptor, I think.

Other than that, I love it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Avians evolved from reptiles, and over time, the differences between the two were pronounced enough that they were in separate taxonomic classes.
This is pretty much the norm in evolution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So that would mean that avians are a subgroup of reptiles? But Aves & Reptilia are both classes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just got weirder and weirder, and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why everyone complains about "crosstest"? It is not like Foundation deliberately demanded that D's carried SCP-1093 inside or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of all the SCP's that made left me befuddled, this one is my favorite. Mainly, because I'm still befuddled, years later.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Not sure if i want to go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm having an issue with the professional tone of the report given that it's based on notes taken by D-class personnel at a strip club. It's particularly noticeable during the The Council of Libidinous Elders scene. The well-informed nature of the record kinda implies an outside observer narrating the acts rather than from our test subject's perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't care for the velociraptors, or 1093. I thought they were both too obviously dangerous. I don't see why on earth a strip-club would kill its patrons. That's terrible for business!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Or equivalent" was a real nice touch.

So, surprisingly, was SCP-1093. I was primed to go all "CROSSTESTING DOWNVOTE SMAAAAAASH!!!" and then it removed the D's internal organs and got me thinking about the nature of SCP-1093 again. Nice.

On SCP-1093, might be good just to note that it is or is not lit up when it appears. I assume it is, but I'd expect the report to explicitly mention it. The fact that it doesn't strike a (slight) false note.

For the same reason you might note what the bouncer is wearing, at least in general terms. Attire of significant entities is usually part of the report.

Minor quibbles both, though. Solid +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the velociraptors were a bit too silly and that the climax on this one could have been more original, but apart from that I like this one a lot. Very weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
or equivalent

Heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I almost imagine this being a rite of passage for MTF agents before graduating to fieldwork. Sounds like a crazy night.

As a reader, I want to see more testing done. As a scientist, I want to see more testing done. Don't do any more testing.

Edit in 2015: Happy +200 upvotes!
Edit in 2019: Happy +300 upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the most entertaining SCPs i've read so far, and i've read a couple hundred, i think.

You were really creative with the whole "sexy in another universe" bit, and I really liked how the whole thing turned out. Weird, yet random enough to be funny. I also really liked the surprise twist with 1093 at the end; I have to admit, I did not see that coming (I did not see any of the acts coming, but i digress.)

+1

item.title: Happy +100 1472
item.content:encoded: I got the 100th upvote. That was pretty well done. But why does the entrance fee have to be blanketed? Seems extraneous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"EXOTIC GIRLS (or equivalent)"

Damn you. To Hell.

And if one can consider this an expansion, I do declare: this is like if Rule 34 was an SCP. Unlike most people, I think my "favorite" was the orangutan. Did't get much creepier than that.

And now I just attempted to +1 it again. Damn you, again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no words for this. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the first part of the article, and I'm thinking, oh, this is an anomalous stripper bar, why is this rated in the 70s.

Then I read the logs, and… jesus.

My only real complaint is that the velociraptors are by far the best entry, and they come in the middle - would have been more effective if you left the velociraptors for last (or near last) or built up to weirder and weirder shit. Instead, you escalate to more physically horrifying/dangerous things. While I like the appearance of 1039, it's not really the showstopper.

Still, upvoted.

item.title: Re: ha ha my vote brings it to +69 lololo
item.content:encoded: I actually like the crosstest…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew this was going to be awesome at 'or equivalent'.

I tried to guess what each one of the dancers was going to be, based on their names.
Needless to say, I failed miserably.

item.title: ha ha my vote brings it to +69 lololo
item.content:encoded:
I really don't like the crosstest.

I really like the rest of the article.

+1 .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That seems kinda unnecessary. What's the point of covering it up, in universe? The building is there for any St. Louis resident to see, and is not obviously anomalous. A cover up just makes it more suspicious. I would change it back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You make a good point. Fixed. Changed the address a bit and added that satellite imaging has been altered to make it appear as an empty lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually wanted more along those lines—the Foundation can't contain everything, and another "relatively harmless, vaguely humanoid, but bloody weird" SCP would have fit the bill, literally. Having said that, I'm not sure the author could have done better than 1093 as a headline act. It just fits this SCP, for some reason.

(I also like how they've apparently only tested this thing once, playing by its stated "rules" at that. Fence it off, it's not worth the trouble of researching.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't want to like it, but I do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the good thing about it, the total randomness. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome, but I do have to wonder what the significance of the 1093 reference is. It seems kind of out of left field and unnecessary. Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Japanese velociraptor stripper maids.

…not a sentence I ever expected to utter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at "or equivalent".

Suggestion: mention somewhere in containment about blocking satellite monitoring. That intersection exists in East St. Louis, but contains:

-an unknown building that is most definitely not brightly painted and does not have a chain link fence

-what sounds like a Chinese restaurant

-"STL Strippers" (?!?!?!)

-an emptyish lot

Only if you want to get meta, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obvious thing I'm wondering about now: does 'Helen Keller' see the notes coming, or does she feel them or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh. Interdimensional stripper joint is best stripper joint!

I was a bit turned off (pun intended) by the crosstest, but the note that 1093 was not present - and that there could be more, potentially-violent members of its kind - shifted me back into upvote territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First a dating site SCP, now a strip club. I like these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 +1 +1
Applause.

Also, it seems kind of abnormal for the Foundation to not blackbox the location information. Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D-Class personnel are the guys on death row that the Foundation uses as test subjects. Not experienced and hardened agents by any sense of the word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked the way that the signage text was collapsed, that was perfect.

Question: Why do D-class personnel involved with testing need to be polygraphed after testing? There is no suggestion that these events in the log would cause experienced and hardened (pun intended) agents would be compelled to lie after testing.

I'm down-voting because the events described in the log were not cohesive. I was disturbed by the end, which is probably the intent, but it was just too random for my taste. Helen Keller, dinosaurs, a computer, disembodied legs, human sacrifice - if it had been any single one of these I would have up-voted. This is just my opinion, others may like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the best I've seen, but dinosaur strippers wins all the upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not so sure about the crosstest, personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm simultaneously baffled and amazed by this SCP. Everything in the test log is just… perfect.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect lolwut. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nHGaKdVStXM

Seriously though, I like the weirdness in this one. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"brothel inside a volcano"

Sounds hot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:|

Well then. Looks like my "brothel inside a volcano" idea has competition.

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.
